title: Dashboard Modelo - RDS - MySQL
description:
This dashboard brings together metrics from the MySQL database engine with resource metrics from the AWS infrastructure for a detailed view of your RDS instances. Further reading on RDS MySQL monitoring:

- [Datadog's guide to key metrics for MySQL on RDS](https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/monitoring-rds-mysql-performance-metrics/)

- [How to collect RDS MySQL metrics](https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/how-to-collect-rds-mysql-metrics/)

- [How to monitor RDS MySQL using Datadog](https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/monitor-rds-mysql-using-datadog/)

- [Datadog's RDS integration docs](https://docs.datadoghq.com/integrations/amazon_rds/)

Clone this template dashboard to make changes and add your own graph widgets. (cloned)

queries.name: query1
queries.query: avg:aws.rds.queries{$region,$dbinstance,$dbcluster,$account}.as_count()

queries.name: query1
queries.query: avg:mysql.performance.threads_connected{$region,$dbinstance}
avg:aws.rds.database_connections{$dbinstance AND $account AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql) AND $region} by {dbclusteridentifier}
queries.name: query1

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.cpuutilization{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)} by {dbinstanceidentifier}
queries.name: query1

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.cpuutilization{$dbinstance AND $account AND $region AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)}
queries.name: query1

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.cpuutilization{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)} by {dbinstanceidentifier}
queries.name: query1

definition.content: Create a [monitor](https://app.datadoghq.com/monitors/create) to get alerted when `aws.rds.cpuutilization` crosses a threshold to help identify performance bottlenecks.

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.freeable_memory{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)} by {dbinstanceidentifier}
queries.name: query1

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.freeable_memory{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)} by {dbinstanceidentifier}
queries.name: query1
query1
queries.query: avg:aws.rds.network_throughput{$dbcluster AND $dbinstance AND $account AND $region AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql*)}

queries.name: query1
queries.query: avg:aws.rds.network_transmit_throughput{$dbcluster AND $dbinstance AND $account AND $region AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)}

queries.name: query1
queries.query: avg:aws.rds.network_receive_throughput{$dbcluster AND $dbinstance AND $account AND $region AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)}

definition.content: A high queue depth can indicate that the storage volume is experiencing a high number of I/O operations, which may be causing performance issues.

definition.title: Instances with Highest Disk Queue Depth

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.disk_queue_depth{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:mysql OR engine:aurora-mysql*)} by {dbinstanceidentifier}
queries.name: query1

definition.content: Forward your [Amazon RDS logs](https://docs.datadoghq.com/integrations/amazon_rds/?tab=standard#log-collection) to Datadog to search, analyze, and troubleshoot issues across multiple RDS instances.

queries.name: query0

queries.query: sum:mysql.queries.errors{$region,$dbinstance} by {query}.as_count()
queries.name: query1

definition.content: Get in-depth understanding of the health and performance of your databases and identify slow queries to troubleshoot by using the Datadog Agent and [Database Monitoring](https://docs.datadoghq.com/database_monitoring/#pagetitle).

queries.query: sum:mysql.queries.count{$dbinstance} by {query,query_signature}.as_rate()
queries.name: query1

queries.query: sum:mysql.queries.time{$dbinstance} by {query,query_signature}.as_rate()
queries.name: query2

queries.query: sum:mysql.queries.time{$dbinstance}.as_count()
queries.name: query3

definition.title: Instances with Lowest Available Disk Space

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.free_storage_space{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:mysql OR engine:aurora-mysql*)} by {dbinstanceidentifier}
queries.name: query1

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.free_storage_space{$account,$region,$dbinstance,$dbcluster} by {dbinstanceidentifier}
queries.name: query1

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.total_storage_space{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)} by {dbinstanceidentifier}
queries.name: query2

definition.content: If the % of free storage space compared to the total storage is running low, it may be necessary to increase the size of the storage volume or take other steps to free up space.

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.write_iops{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)}
queries.name: query1

queries.name: query1

queries.query: max:aws.rds.swap_usage{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)}
queries.name: query1

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.read_iops{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)}
queries.name: query1

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.read_iops{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:mysql OR engine:aurora-mysql)} by {dbinstanceidentifier}
queries.name: query1

queries.query: avg:aws.rds.read_iops{$account AND $region AND $dbinstance AND $dbcluster AND (engine:aurora-mysql OR engine:mysql)} by {dbinstanceidentifier}.as_rate()
queries.name: query1

definition.content: If the swap usage is high, it may indicate that the instance is running low on available memory and is using swap space to store data that is normally stored in memory.
